data.title: As a trigger implementation, I can scope sg structural analysis to staged Python files via diff-to-sg-scope.sh with graceful fallback
data.description:
## Description

**What**: diff-to-sg-scope.sh wrapper script that scopes sg structural analysis to staged Python files, with graceful fallback when sg is absent.
**Why**: Triggers T5 and T11 (Story E) need structural pattern matching on staged files. This wrapper isolates sg invocation, absence detection, and NDJSON parsing behind a single interface with a formal output contract.
**Scope**:
- IN: diff-to-sg-scope.sh script with formal exit-code and output-format contract; sg NDJSON parsing; absence detection; first-commit guard
- OUT: T5 and T11 trigger logic (Story E); grep fallback implementation (Story E consumes the contract)

## Done Definitions

- When this story is complete, diff-to-sg-scope.sh exits 2 when sg is absent (command -v sg pre-check; not exit code check)
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-10
- When this story is complete, diff-to-sg-scope.sh exits 2 when no HEAD~1 exists (first commit guard)
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-10
- When this story is complete, the formal output contract is implemented and documented: exit codes (0=no matches, 1=matches found, 2=sg absent or no HEAD~1); stdout format is NDJSON one JSON object per line with fields: path, range, text, rule_id; grep-fallback output format is documented for Story E consumers
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-10
- When this story is complete, sg --json=stream NDJSON output is parsed correctly against the schema confirmed in Spike S0
  <- Satisfies: SC-E1-10
- When this story is complete, unit tests written and passing for all new or modified logic

## Escalation Policy

**Escalation policy**: Escalate to the user whenever you do not have high confidence in your understanding of the work, approach, or intent. High confidence means clear evidence from the codebase or ticket context — not inference or reasonable assumption. When in doubt, stop and ask rather than guess.
